steps.title: Find the interior angle at the 130° vertex
steps.content: The angle marked 130° is an exterior angle of the triangle formed by the two transversals and the parallel lines. The interior angle at that vertex is the supplement: $$180° - 130° = 50°$$

steps.title: Identify the angle between the two transversals at line m using co-interior angles
steps.content: When transversal j crosses parallel lines m and n, the co-interior angles sum to 180°. The angle at line n (interior to the triangle) is 50°, so the angle j makes with line m is also 50° by alternate interior angles. Similarly, transversal k makes an angle b° with line n. Since these transversals meet at line m, the interior angle of the triangle at their intersection is the sum of both angles: $$(50° + b°)$$

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum to solve for b
steps.content:
The three interior angles of the triangle must sum to 180°:
$$50° + b° + (50° + b°) = 180°$$
$$100° + 2b° = 180°$$
$$2b° = 80°$$
$$b° = 40°$$

We can verify this using the exterior angle theorem: the exterior angle (130°) equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: $130° = (50° + b°) + b° = 50° + 2(40°) = 130°$ ✓

That matches choice A — A is correct.
